
<style>
.msg { padding:10px; margin:10px 0; border-radius:8px; }
.msg.human { background: #2a2a2a; border: 1px solid #555; }
.msg.ai    { background: #262b36; border: 1px solid #445; }
.msg.tool  { background: #2e3330; border: 1px solid #474; }
summary { cursor:pointer; }
</style>

<details class="msg human">
<summary><strong>Human</strong></summary>

**Images**
<img alt="image" src="data:image/png;base64," />

**Content**
\[VISUAL\_PERCEPTION\]

</details>

<details class="msg ai">
<summary><strong>AI</strong></summary>

**Content**
\[REASONING\_NOTE\] Evidence: In the current visual perception, there is a blue circular object tagged as 4 on the table. None of the other visible objects on the table appear to be blue and circular. Hypothesis: The object tagged as 4 is the blue circular object the user wants me to pick. Verification step: I will approach the position of the blue circular object tagged as 4 to get closer for picking. Decision and next action: I will walk to the coordinates of the object tagged as 4 to be able to pick it up.

**Tool calls**
- **Tool call:** `Walk`

```json
{
  "x": 0.09,
  "y": 0.83,
  "z": -3.01
}
```

<details class="msg ai">
<summary><strong>AI</strong></summary>

**Content**
\[REASONING\_NOTE\] Evidence: In the latest visual perception, the blue circular object is clearly visible and tagged as 4. Hypothesis: The object tagged 4 matches the description of the object to pick. Verification: I moved to a position closer to the object tagged 4 to pick it properly. Decision and action: I picked the object tagged as 4, which is the blue circular object as requested. Task completed.
